title: mallotus claoxyloides var. cordatus
intro: Mallotus claoxyloides var. cordatus, also known as the Yellow Bells or Yellow Rangoli, is a species of plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Mallotus claoxyloides var. cordatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, or used in herbal preparations such as capsules or tinctures.
